name: atlantis-android
description: Capture HTTP/HTTPS traffic from Android apps and send to Proxyman for debugging.
readme_summary: Atlantis Android is a library that captures and inspects HTTP/HTTPS and WebSocket traffic from Android applications. It seamlessly integrates with OkHttp and popular libraries like Retrofit and Apollo, sending the captured data to Proxyman for debugging without requiring proxy configuration or certificate installation.
key_features: Compatibility with Retrofit 2.9+ and Apollo Kotlin 3.x/4.x
Network Service Discovery (NSD) for automatic Proxyman detection
primary_use_case: Debugging network traffic from Android applications by capturing and sending it to Proxyman.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly, stylized robot detective holding a magnifying glass over a flowing stream of digital data packets. The robot is carefully examining the packets, with some packets glowing red to indicate potential issues and others glowing green as they pass inspection. In the background, a stylized representation of an Android phone shows a simplified network activity graph. Subtle code snippets and the 'Atlantis' name are integrated into the scene's environment. The overall mood is one of clarity and efficient problem-solving, rendered in a clean, 3D isometric illustration style with a bright, tech-focused color palette.

name: arozos
description: Web Desktop Operating System for low power platforms, Now written in Go!
readme_summary: ArozOS is a web-based desktop operating system designed for low-power platforms like the Raspberry Pi. It provides a user-friendly interface accessible via a web browser, offering features for file management, networking, and basic system settings, with extensibility through scripting and module development.
key_features: Build-in Network File Sharing Servers (FTP, WebDAV, SFTP)
Low system memory and storage requirements
primary_use_case: To provide a functional and user-friendly desktop-like experience on low-power devices, accessible remotely through a web browser, enabling file management, network services, and cloud storage integration.
cover_image_prompt: A miniature, stylized Raspberry Pi computer sits on a desk, but instead of a traditional screen, a vibrant, interactive web browser window floats above it. Inside the browser window, a clean, organized desktop interface is displayed, complete with file icons, a taskbar, and a startup menu, all rendered in a modern, flat design. Small, glowing icons representing cloud storage (like Google Drive), network shares, and file transfer protocols (FTP, WebDAV) orbit the main desktop window, illustrating seamless connectivity and data management. Subtle digital particles emanate from the Raspberry Pi, connecting it to the browser interface, emphasizing its role as the core processing unit for this web-based OS. The overall scene is bright, clean, and futuristic, rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style with a focus on clarity and user-friendliness.

name: gmail-cleaner
description: web based GUI to cleanup gmail delete, mark as read, unsubsribe from uncessary things u dont like
readme_summary: Gmail Cleaner is a free, privacy-focused, self-hosted web application designed to help users manage their Gmail inbox. It allows for bulk operations such as unsubscribing from emails, deleting emails by sender, marking emails as read, archiving, and managing labels, all while running locally on the user's machine.
primary_use_case: Bulk cleaning and management of Gmail inbox by unsubscribing, deleting, archiving, and labeling emails.
cover_image_prompt: A digital librarian meticulously organizing a vast, chaotic library of glowing email scrolls. The librarian uses a special tool that emits beams of light, sorting the scrolls into neat stacks labeled 'Unsubscribe', 'Delete', 'Archive', and 'Read'. Some scrolls are being gently pushed away, representing unwanted emails. In the background, a subtle, clean UI interface shows progress bars and filter options. The overall mood is one of calm efficiency and digital order, with a focus on privacy. The art style is a 3D isometric illustration with a clean, modern aesthetic and a soft, blue-toned color palette.

name: DriveLite
description: DriveLite: The Supabase for File Storage. A modular, self-hostable backend with end-to-end encryption.
readme_summary: DriveLite is a fast, minimalist, and privacy-focused file storage application that offers both self-hosting and a free cloud instance. It provides end-to-end encryption, file sharing, and synchronization capabilities, acting as a secure alternative to traditional cloud storage solutions.
primary_use_case: Providing a secure, private, and self-hostable file storage solution with end-to-end encryption.
cover_image_prompt: A secure, transparent vault made of glowing digital energy, floating in a minimalist space. Inside the vault, files are neatly organized and protected by a shimmering shield. A single, stylized keyhole on the vault's exterior subtly glows, representing access control. Around the vault, abstract data streams flow in and out, symbolizing upload and download, but they are all encrypted and unreadable to any outside observer. Small, subtle UI elements resembling a file explorer and encryption icons are integrated into the background. The overall mood is one of security, privacy, and digital control. Rendered in a clean, modern 3D isometric illustration style with a cool blue and white color palette, emphasizing clarity and technological sophistication.

name: ArchivedV
description: Tracking and Saving Unarchived Singing from Youtube Stream
readme_summary: ArchivedV is a JavaScript-based service designed to automatically download and save singing streams from YouTube, particularly those by Vtubers, which are often unarchived due to copyright concerns. It allows users to filter streams by keywords, manage subscriptions via a web interface, and includes features for handling stream interruptions and sending notifications.
key_features: Automatic checking of subscribed channels for new live streams
Web interface for managing channels and keywords
Fail-safe auto-merger using ffmpeg for incomplete downloads
Support for members-only/private videos via cookies
primary_use_case: To automatically capture and archive unarchived singing streams from YouTube, primarily for Vtubers, ensuring content is preserved despite potential copyright issues or temporary unarchiving.
cover_image_prompt: A diligent archivist, personified as a wise owl wearing a small headset, sits at a desk filled with glowing YouTube stream icons. The owl is meticulously sorting and saving vibrant, musical notes that are falling from a projected YouTube live stream onto a digital ledger. In the background, a subtle, stylized web interface shows lists of channels and keywords. Small, ethereal data streams flow from the live stream icon to the owl's ledger, symbolizing the capture of unarchived singing content. The scene is set in a cozy, dimly lit study with a hint of digital magic. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a warm, inviting color palette, emphasizing clarity and preservation.

name: dockmon
description: DockMon - Modern Docker container monitoring with auto-restart and alerts
readme_summary: DockMon is a comprehensive platform for monitoring and managing Docker containers across multiple hosts. It offers real-time dashboards, intelligent auto-restart, advanced alerting, and detailed event logging, with features for stack management and secure remote monitoring.
primary_use_case: Monitoring and managing Docker containers across multiple hosts with features like auto-restart, alerting, and stack deployment.
cover_image_prompt: A vigilant, multi-eyed guardian robot overseeing a bustling cityscape of interconnected Docker containers. Each container is represented by a unique, glowing icon. The guardian is actively directing traffic, with streams of data flowing from containers to a central console displaying real-time metrics like CPU and memory usage. Some containers are being gently nudged back into place by robotic arms, symbolizing auto-restart. Small alert icons flash on a secondary screen, indicating notifications being sent to various communication channels like Slack and Discord. The overall scene is clean, modern, and slightly futuristic, with a focus on order and control within the container ecosystem. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a vibrant, tech-focused color palette.
